candidates.notes: Meets required packet shape and stays source-bound. U1 covers decoupling, thermal/exposed-pad/vias/ground, and EVAL/reference-layout findings with bounded citations. U2 reuses datasheet cache, records local EVAL none_found, uses the approved mocked supplemental app note, and ties final readiness to the supplemental page. J1 remains review_required with no invented guidance. Figure citation dedupe and table rejection policy are handled correctly. Slightly less explicit than the medium candidate in a few manifest/rejection details, but still above pass threshold.

Best quality-complete packet. Satisfies all hard gates with the clearest traceability, rejection reasoning, figure/table policy handling, and readiness discipline. U1 and U2 coverage is complete and well-bounded; J1 is correctly blocked. Manifest content is the strongest of the set while remaining synthetic and boundary-safe.

critical_errors: Fails table_policy by promoting U1_table_eval_p7_jumpers even though board setup/jumper context must remain non-promoted/context-only.
Fails figure/table policy consistency because the same U1 jumper table is treated as promoted rather than rejected/context-only across packet sections.
candidates.notes: Otherwise strong on source traceability and boundary handling, but the promoted U1 jumper/setup table is a hard-gate failure. Higher cost cannot be considered because it fails policy.

critical_errors: Fails table_policy by promoting U1_table_eval_p7_jumpers; setup/jumper context must not be promoted as final layout table guidance.
Fails figure/table policy consistency by adding a cited table page pair for the promoted U1 jumper table.
Fails portable_paths_only due to including concrete calibration_id in extraction_manifest.
candidates.notes: Covers much of the required content, but hard-gate failures on table promotion and non-portable concrete run id disqualify it. It also reads less disciplined than the passing 5.4 candidates.

notes: Usable ranking includes only passing shadows. Between the two passers, B_gpt_5_4_low is preferred for calibration use because it clears the hard gates and minimum quality bar while likely being lower token/time cost than B_gpt_5_4_medium. B_gpt_5_4_medium is the highest-quality output overall, but not enough better to outweigh expected extra cost for a shadow rank focused on usable quality-passing efficiency. Do not select either 5.5 candidate because both fail hard policy gates, primarily table-promotion discipline.
